entry.title: Direct Network Path Backup in SAP Business One HANA DB Server
entry.content:
Dear SAP Tech Users,

I have a question regarding SAP Business One HANA DB Server backup. Is it possible to configure a direct network path backup in RSP Backup, such as \\127.0.0.1\Backup Path? If not, is there any other SAP inbuilt utility that can solve this problem and allow the backup to go directly to a network-located NAS drive?

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate Testing Workstream
entry.content:
Hi guys, please I'm doing my certification and I have a doubt in one question about test automation toll for S/4HANA Cloud, public edition. Please, anyone can help me with the correct answer.

The question is:

Which of the following are capabilities of the test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition? Select 2 alternatives.
1) Raises test defects automatically
2) Supports recording of transaction-based processes
3) Supports the creation od custom test automates
4) Captures test results with screenshot

In my opinion the correct answer is options 3 and 4. But I found another forum with the option 2 is correct too.
Please, anyone can help me with this question?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: IPS Delta Read
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have defined the following properties in the Source System (Azure): ips.full.read.force.count=3.

In the Target System (Identity Authentication), I have defined this property: ips.delete.existedbefore.entities=true.

Then, I deleted a group in IAS. I thought that when the READ job is executed 3 times (which would be Delta Read), a Full Read would be triggered on the 4th run, and the deleted group would be re-provisioned from the Source System to the Target System.

However, this is not happening.

What am I doing wrong?

How can I schedule the Delta Read job? I cannot find the ips.delta.read=enebaled parameter in either the Source or the Target system.

Thank you very much

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: The Future of Compatibility Packs in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a question towards the use of the Cost-of-Sales ledger (table GLFUNCT). I understand that this is also part of the Compatibility Pack until 31 Dec 2025 - my question is if it is really necessary to find an alternative way to build up reporting based on a P&L by functional area (in other words: rebuild reports, programs,... to read from ACDOCA instead of GLFUNCT)?

We are using GLFUNCT only - so only summarized data, but no line items for ledger 0F. Currently on:
S4HANA ON PREMISE 2022 01 (02/2023) FPS (private cloud - RISE), with an upgrade to 2023 FPS02 planned in Q2/2025.

We are using GLFUNCT data only for reporting purposes (a GR53 report built on library 0M1 and a couple of Z-reports - which are (unfortunately 😉  ) VERY relevant for our month-end closing process).

My understanding during the S/4 implementation project (brown-field approach, from ECC6.0 / classical G/L accounting  to S/4HANA Universal Journal with account-based multiple-GAAP approach, only calculatoric CO-PA) was that GLFUNCT is still populated (which is true, we just had to adapt a few settings, like making sure that KSU5/KSV5 cycle postings are reflected in GLFUNCT correctly - which was not necessary in ECC6.0 since transaction KALC took care of functional area "shifts"), it just won't be further developed by SAP and support for it will stop after 31 Dec. 2025.

I am aware that changing the above mentioned reports to use ACDOCA instead will be necessary at some point in time - I am just afraid that this point in time will already be 31 Dec 2025, since there is already a full project pipeline ahead of us 🙂


Thanks & have a nice day,
Benni

entry.title: Re: How to get the total number of data point comments in...
entry.content:
Thanks @N1kh1l 

Actually I got that in the model preferences there is comment statistics which shows total count of the comments.

There are nearly 1000 comments which has to be transported now from one tenant to another but as we discussed in earlier questions transporting is not possible

So I am really struggling on this comment transport solution

entry.title: Do you know which services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition are performed by SAP?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Administering SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Interaction with SAP learning journey.

Overview
After completing this learning journey, the learners will be able to identify which services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition are performed by SAP, and which services can only be performed by the customer. Additionally, they will understand the use cases for SAP Service requests including their process flow and how to create Service Requests to interact with SAP, what input SAP requires to accomplish an ordered task, and what output to expect.

Learning objectives
This course will prepare to:

  • Explain the meaning of the Roles and Responsibilities document
  • Classify services in the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Explain the use of SAP Cloud Application Services
  • Illustrate the Service Process Flow using examples.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP HANA DB Server Installation on SUSE Linux
entry.content:
Dear SAP Tech Users,

I need to know that, while installing SAP HANA DB SERVER on SUSE Linux, does it install both the application and database server on a single server, or does it only install the database server and the application server remains on a Windows server? Additionally, what web server services does it use on SUSE Linux or any other Linux platform? Does it use Apache, Nginx, or a self-built web server, and where is it located? What is the web service or web server name?

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Does anyone know where I can download compass example...
entry.content:
@SemJon 

Looking at the help page it looks like the Best Bike Model. May be you could try the sample planning model in Content network. You might have to adapt a bit.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: how to handle unplanned freight cost
entry.content: Scenario: in our system, user created PR, PR go through the approval flow, after PR released, PR will be converted to PO, supplier delivered products and sent the invoice which included the freight cost, this freight cost was not included in PO. Question: how do we handle the unplanned freight cost? we don't want to PO go through the approval flow again, and slow down the invoice posting?

entry.title: Re: Can we schedule a standalone bonus calculation recurr...
entry.content:
Hi 

Yes, you can schedule a standalone recurring job for Set Bonus Calculation in VarPay. Use the Set Bonus Calculation job in SuccessFactors Compensation to trigger bonus calculations for all employees, regardless of EC record changes. Schedule it separately from the ECT History Processor job at your preferred frequency. Ensure it runs after any updates to business goals, weights, or bonus plans to reflect the latest changes. Configure the job in Provisioning or Admin Center under Compensation Home > Scheduled Jobs.

entry.title: SAC - Calculated measure in a hierarchy
entry.content:
Hello community


I'm new here and need some help. Is there a way to make a calculated measure where its multiply the leafs members in a hierarchy? I didn't find any product function to use. Should i make a data action for it?

entry.title: How to Switch from GET to POST Call in SAPUI5 Analytical Table with Node.js Backend?
entry.content:
Hello SCN community,

I am working with an SAPUI5 Analytical Table bound to a Node.js backend service using analytical binding, and everything is working as expected with the default GET request. However, I now need to change the default behavior to use a POST request instead of GET in order to send additional data along with the request.

Current Setup:

var oModel = this.getView().getModel("analyticalModel");
oModel._request = odataModelRequest(oModel._request, this._waitODataLongReq.bind(this));

var oBindingParams = {
entitySet: "dynamicEntity",
useAnalyticalBinding: true,
initiallyVisibleFields: '',
useBatchRequests: false,
provideGrandTotals: true,
hasTotaledMeasures: true,
provideTotalResultSize: true,
reloadSingleUnitMeasures: true,
sOperationMode: sap.ui.model.odata.OperationMode.Server,
autoExpandMode: 'Sequential',
custom: { // Adding custom query parameters here
params: sParams
}
};

let fecBindcontext = {
model: oModel,
path: "/dynamicEntity"
};

var spath = `/storage/sys/${this._UUID}/se11/${this.tables}/dynamicEntity`;
var oTableBinding = new sap.ui.model.analytics.AnalyticalBinding(oModel, spath, fecBindcontext, [], [], oBindingParams);
oTable.setModel(oModel);

 

My Questions:

  1. Does the SAPUI5 Analytical Table support POST requests?
  2. If yes, could someone guide me on how to implement this?
    • Specifically, as it is a direct binding, I need to know how to modify the request type and how to pass the data (which is in another tab) in the body of the POST request to fetch the data in the View Output tab

      Any help or examples would be greatly appreciated!

      Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: Classes not showing in date order
entry.content:
Hi 

The class sorting in SAP SuccessFactors LMS may not always default to chronological order.
The order is typically influenced by system sorting logic, which can be based on Class ID, Offering ID, or a backend sorting mechanism.

To fix this, check these settings in LMS Admin:
1. Admin Center > Learning Administration > System Configuration > LMS_ADMIN – Look for sorting-related settings.
2. Class List View Configuration – Ensure sorting is set to Start Date (ascending or descending).
3. Reports & Queries – If using a custom report, verify the sorting logic in the query.

If sorting still appears incorrect, consider logging a case with SAP Support for further investigation.

entry.title: Re: Use Date input control in a SAC formula to calculate...
entry.content: Hello N1kh1l, apart date input control, is possible in a formula, to calculate a date difference, asking to the user to fill a date on the webpage to use as a "To date" in the formula? That is, is not possible any way to calculate a date difference having only a starting date, and getting the "To date" somewhere where the user can set it? Thank you

Alex

entry.title: HANA SQL QUERY TO FETCH NESTED JSON OBJECTS
entry.content:
hi ,

I have a table machine_group with values as shown.

 

Now using a query as show below

select * from machine_group for json

the values fetched as below 

[
  {"COLA" :"A","COLB":"VAL1"},{"COLA" :"A","COLB":"VAL2"},{"COLA" :"A","COLB":"VAL3"},
  {"COLA" :"B","COLB":"VAL5"},{"COLA" :"B","COLB":"VAL6"},{"COLA" :"B","COLB":"VAL7"}
 ]

But am looking to fetch the output in nested json format as shown below.

 
[
{
"COLA":"A",
"COLB":[ {"COLB":"VAL1"}, {"COLB":"VAL2"}, {"COLB":"VAL3"}]
},
{
"COLA":"B",
"COLB":[ {"COLB":"VAL4"}, {"COLB":"VAL5"}, {"COLB":"VAL6"}]
}
]

 

Please help me. 

Regards

Govardan

entry.title: Re: Business Process Integration certificate
entry.content:
Hi @ManojRaju
this looks like a new question.
Can you please post it in our SAP Learning Q&A

This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses
Would you mind to repost it ?

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: CCM - Statistical price condition in delta accruals
entry.content:
Hi 

Your issue likely stems from the missing Accrual flag in RED1 and incorrect account determination. Try these steps:
1. Ensure RED1 is correctly mapped to REA1 in the pricing procedure and flagged as Accruals.
2. Activate the accrual flag for RED1 in V/06 (Condition Type Configuration) to ensure it behaves like REA1.
3. Verify the account determination settings in T-code OBYC (Transaction Key for Accruals) to ensure accrual-related accounts are triggered.
4. Check the posting configuration in 0S42, ensuring Posting Statistical Condition to Accounting is correctly set.
5. If the FI document does not generate, run ACDOCA checks to confirm statistical postings are processed.
6. Test with a new pricing procedure if issues persist, ensuring statistical conditions are correctly assigned.

entry.title: Re: PCR for OT calculations
entry.content:
I don't think you need separate time types which puts the burden back on the employee or end user recording the time. Try using VARSTDAYTY to determine if it is a scheduled or unscheduled day. Then you can use FILLP to change the valuation type.

I hope this helps. 

Cindy

entry.title: Re: SAC - Calculated measure in a hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi @priscilla_barbosa82 

You can use the lookup function in the model calculated measures to make use of the leaf nodes in hierarchy.

You can also have the account dimension where Formula column is readily available for it.

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: Re: Memories of the Newtown Square Campus
entry.content:
@jerryjanda Thank you for your share and compassion for the history of the Lyondell buildings, SAP Campus including NSQ1 and NSQ2.   The grounds, buildings and expansion in NSQ is truly bittersweet.   Yes, the geese are also a hazard.  

I remember my first snowfall at NSQ.  I live in NJ and drove the treacherous winding roads of Bishop Hallow to NSQ (prior to the 1/2 being added).   The snow was falling and the landscape untouched.   I remember thinking to myself this is my vision of Robert Frost's poem "Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening".

entry.title: Re: Import existing BPM source code project NWDS
entry.content:
Hello ;

You can perform the following steps via the NWDS application.
Create a new SC under the Development Infrastructure view

 

 

 

The SC you created will look like this:

 

Delete the resources in SC by selecting them from the root section

In the final case, your SC will appear empty as below.

 

 

Extract your own source codes to a folder and import these resources to nwds from the import section below.

 

 

 

When you complete the above operations and click the finish button, the following screen will open.

After selecting Yes, you can add your source files under the SC you created before.

 


 

In the last case, you can see your own source files under SC. After the build process, your bpm will be ready under the process development view.

Once the above process is completed, switch to the Process development view.

 

Your BPM is ready,

Regards

Hasan

entry.title: Re: Eclipse(HANA Development): Error when searching for a...
entry.content: Pessoal, talvez não seja a melhor solução, mas resolveu meu problema... Eu simplesmente desinstalei tudo do meu eclipse, incluindo o workspace e instalei outro IDE. Estava usando o que está destacado em vermelho na versão 2024-09 quando deu pau. Instalei o que está em verde na mesma versão 2024-09 e deu boa. Espero que de alguma forma ajude.

entry.title: Re: SAP Reference Architecture Framework (SEAF) & RBA
entry.content:
Hi Lorimer1,

thanks a lot for your post. I am aware that the current delay in updating the SAP Reference Architecture in Signavio compared to LeanIX causes issues for you. We are looking for possibilities to tackle this problem.

If you want to engage with us on the SAP Reference Architecture and its representation in LeanIX and Signavio please consider to subscribe to the customer engagement initiative "SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX". This will be published on influence.sap.com and the registration is planned to be open from Feb 24th to March 28th. Please feel also free to reach out directly to me under martin.effenberger@sap.com

Best regards

Martin

entry.title: SAC Composites - Retrieve/Update Interface Properties at script level
entry.content:
Is there anyway to access the properties you create for a composite at script level?

I'm only seeing functions we've defined when using COMPOSITE_1.x, and we cant update the properties within the functions themselves when defining the composite. Would make more sense to update the properties that are bindable.

API response

If not this approach, how else can we check if data is available in MDI?

Regards,
Indu

entry.title: Security Integration Test for CAP Java App doesn't consider Jwt Token
entry.content:
We want to build a new app based on CAP Java. Currently we try to implement the CAP exits for on-/offboarding and offer a CDS entity for users with a specific scope.

When we deploy the app to a sandbox and do an onboarding and request the data of the CDS entity everything works fine (if fails if no/wrong scope is given with the expected http response and returns data with correct scope).

But when we implement integration tests as Spring Boot Test to check the security config, they end up with incorrect results:

  • If we don't pass any Jwt token to the onboarding endpoint, we get a 403 (Forbidden) but not a 401 (Unauthorized)
  • If we pass an incorrect Jwt token to the read endpoint of the CDS entity, we get a 401 (Unauthorized) but not a 403 (Forbidden)

Especially the last case works correctly in the real deployment.

When trying to debug the second case (reading data of the CDS entity) I have seen that some kind of test user "anonymous" is automatically used by CAP and the Jwt token seems to be ignored. Does anyone know what we need to do so that the Jwt token is considered instead?

Unfortunately I have no clue which information would be helpful for the analysis 😞

  • We use Java 17
  • CDS Services 3.6.0
  • SAP Cloud SDK 5.15.0
  • SAP Cloud Security 3.5.7
  • org.springframework.web.reactive.function.client.WebClient
  • com.sap.cloud.security.test.JwtGenerator
  • and have configured sap.security.services.xsuaa in the test application.yaml

The respective branch in our git repo can be found here: https://github.wdf.sap.corp/GRC-CH/FinancialComplianceLaunchpad/tree/on_off_boarding

The test case is https://github.wdf.sap.corp/GRC-CH/FinancialComplianceLaunchpad/blob/on_off_boarding/srv/src/test/java/com/sap/grc/ram/api/subscription/AuthorizationIT.java

entry.title: Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
This blog shares highlights on Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.

  • Ability To Customize Cancellation Reasons
  • Enhanced Change Capabilities For Mass Update Application For Service Contracts
  • Debit Memo Request For Service Contract Invoices
  • Add Fields to Form Templates for Service Quotation
  • Send Service Quotations for Workflow Approval using OData API

Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

Ability To Customize Cancellation Reasons

One of the standout features in this release is the ability to customize cancellation reasons. In response to numerous customer and partner requests, SAP now enables you to add custom cancellation reasons that reflect more specific insights about why contracts are terminated. This not only aids in understanding customer needs better but also helps in refining service delivery.

You can now choose customized cancellation reasons when you cancel service contracts. This flexibility helps you understand why customers decide to cancel the contracts. Using the cancellation reasons, you can make data-driven improvements.

Key users and configuration experts can now make settings for service contract cancellations with two new configuration activities:

First, you can define the cancelling party. This means you can designate which parties are authorized to cancel a contract.

Second, you can define cancellation reasons. This provides the ability to set and assign specific reasons for contract cancellations to a cancelling party.

Understanding specific cancellation reasons allows businesses to address customer concerns proactively, reducing churn and enhancing service offerings based on informed decision-making. 

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

Demo: Ability to Customize Cancellation Reasons

 

Enhanced Change Capabilities For Mass Update Application For Service Contracts

Building on enhancements introduced in previous releases, 2502 brings robust capabilities for mass updates of service contracts. These include changing partner roles and payment terms/methods en masse, making it easier to adapt to large-scale customer requests or policy changes efficiently.

You can now change billing-relevant data, such as the payment method and terms of payment, at both the header and item levels. In addition, you can change business partners at the header and item levels.

You can change standard partner roles, such as the employee responsible, though this does not include the sold-to party.

You also have the ability to change payment terms and methods efficiently. This update ensures significant time savings and operational efficiency by enabling bulk changes at both the service contract header and item levels. Whether you are adjusting employee responsibilities or modifying payment methods across multiple contracts, these capabilities offer seamless process management.

By automating mass updates, organizations save time and reduce manual errors, increasing operational efficiency and freeing resources for strategic tasks.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

Demo: Enhanced Change Capabilities For Mass Update Application For Service Contracts

 

Debit Memo Request For Service Contract Invoices

The ability to issue debit memo requests for service contract invoices is another feature designed to enhance financial operations. If discrepancies such as unpaid service orders arise or additional services are rendered, you can effortlessly create debit memo requests to notify customers of outstanding payments.

With this feature, you can create debit memo requests that were created with reference to service invoices. A debit memo request is a sales document that can be used to request a debit memo for a customer. Creating debit memo requests makes sense when the price that the customer has paid was too low. This could be due to wrongly calculated prices. After a debit memo request is created, you can find it in the transaction history of the corresponding service transaction. From there, for service contracts only, you can also navigate to relevant apps for more details about the document.

After the debit memo requests are created, they will show up in the Process Flow in the referenced service contracts. 

Figure: Debit Memo Request For Service Contract Invoices


In addition, SAP now allows these debit memo requests to be efficiently uploaded via spreadsheets, catering to businesses dealing with a high volume of transactions. This functionality will streamline operations and ensure timely follow-up on client invoices. Streamlined financial processes improve cash flow management and facilitate prompt customer interactions, enhancing financial transparency and reliability.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

Demo: Debit Memo Request For Service Contract Invoices

 

Add Fields to Form Templates for Service Quotation

The previous standard form template lacks flexibility with the output forms for Service Quotations. The standard template is not designed on S/4HANA Output Management. That makes it difficult to access key information in service quotations. 

Originally, the standard form template uses a non-standard wrapper.  Now it is replaced by a common wrapper created for service objects. The new wrapper can be applied to Service Quotations.

 
With this enhancement, customers are able to add more fields to the form template as output for service quotations. With the new fields in the output form, customers have better clarity in the detailed terms of agreement in the service quotations. This leaves no room for confusion in the complicated quotation processes.

This is an example of the standard output form of a service quotation.

After adding new fields to the output form, the output form looks like the following. 

Send Service Quotations for Workflow Approval using OData API

Before this enhancement, the OData API cannot trigger the approval workflow for service quotations. Customers can only use the button "Send for Approval" in the Manage Service Quotations app to trigger the approval workflow. Lacking the capability in the OData API makes the approval process time consuming.

This was resolved by adding the Action "SubmitForApproval" in the ODATA API for Service Quotation that can be used by external integrations.

The enhancement in the OData API helps customers trigger approval workflow for service quotations when they use integration and custom UI5 Apps. Users do not need to open the Manage Service Quotations app and trigger the workflow manually. It saves you time and effort. 

Here is the documentation for the API: Service Quotation (A2X)

Here is the process flow for the service quotation approval using the OData API.

Figure: Send Quotation for Workflow Approval for Service Quotations using OData API

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

 

Discover the comprehensive learning resources for Service:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Service

The following Resource Map offers you a comprehensive collection of learning materials from business processes and Lines of Business (LoB).

Resource Map

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Central Business Configuration
  • Extensibility
  • Two-Tier ERP
  • Automation Testing
  • Intelligent ERP
  • Master Data
  • Road to Success
  • Localization and Identity and Access Management

Recruit to Retire Human Resources

Idea to Market R&D and Engineering | Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

Acquire to Decommission Asset Management

Source to Pay Sourcing and Procurement

Lead to Cash Sales | Solution Order Management | Service | Professional Services

Plan to Fulfill Manufacturing | Supply Chain

Finance | Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC), Trade, and Tax

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series! 

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
@Suresh54 check out the gitrepository https://github.com/peasantsboot/ProcessIntegrationPipelineSampleScenarios to get an idea how such scenarios are setup.

In general, the following needs to be setup:

  • As a prerequisite, deploy all generic flows from the provided package
  • Create scenario-specific inbound processing, inbound conversion and outbound processing flows
  • Create a new partner for the scenario in the Partner Directory and maintain the partner directory artifacts such as max retry, the end point to the inbound conversion flow if any, the XSLTs for receiver determination and interface determination, alternative IDs to map sender and sender interface to the scenario

If you migrate from SAP Process Orchestration, you can use the migration tool in future, we actually plan to automatically create the Pipeline artifacts via the migration wizard, so stay tuned

Alex

entry.title: Re: Can we schedule a standalone bonus calculation recurr...
entry.content:
Hi

Thanks for the clarification. Since Provisioning does not allow creating a Set Bonus Calculation job, and Compensation Home only allows monitoring jobs, here are your options:

1. Use Integration Center:
Create a custom job to trigger bonus recalculation for all employees.
Schedule it to run at the desired frequency.

2. Leverage Business Rules:
Set up a rule to trigger bonus recalculation based on changes to business goals, weights, or bonus plans.

3. Check with SAP Support:
Some customers require an SAP ticket to enable the Set Bonus Calculation job as a standalone option.
Request backend activation if needed. If Integration Center is not an option, SAP Support is your best path forward.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors LMS - Microsoft Teams VLS Integration
entry.content:
Hello Simon, 

 

  You can check this, i found this note https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3304371

Thank you,

Rana

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation – SMTP Mail Destination using Microsoft 365 email provider
entry.content:
Hi @KarthikeyanMurthy,

Apologies for the delay reaponse, as you informed I have collected few images of Monitoring -> Sign-in Logs from Azure tenant.

So, please suggest me some solution for this error, what exactly is missing in this SMTP Configaration and what I need to do to solve this error.





Pov  :      In username   -   I have provided mailbox mail id,
               Password    -    I have provided mailbox password(Login Password),
               In sender address   -   I have given mailbox mail id(mailbox mail id),
               Scope    -     https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.send



Thanks & regards,
Ajith E

entry.title: Re: Central Contract without company code.
entry.content:
It is not possible to create a contract in SAP without a company code by standard design.

Read SAPs KBA / note released for it - https://me.sap.com/notes/0002675635

entry.title: How to do the operating procedure on the configuration of bridge days and public holidays not provid
entry.content: Comment faire la procédure d’exploitation sur la configuration des jours de pont et des jours fériés non prévus à l’avance par la législation sur SAP

entry.title: 49P scope item, missing sample data for SAC workshop
entry.content:
Hello,

We have activated scope item 49P content, but no sample data is available. The documentation mention that the scope item is delivered with preconfigured story samples. This means that only includes the models but not any data. We really need to connect the SAC starter with S4Hana starter system? 

I'm a little concern that the workshop data will depend on the poor data available in the starter system.

Can you please share your experience?

Kind regards,

Sandra

entry.title: Pre-check "Complete assigned notifications" on "Find maintenance order" app
entry.content:
Hello,

while doing completion of orders using button available on above ribbon on "Find maintenance order" app, checkbox for completing assigned notifications is not pre-checked.

 

Is there a way to have this checkbox checked by default?

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Does SAC have a Rank function?
entry.content: I do not want to sort max and min but want the rank randomly. For a given measure column I want to have another column that ranks the values in the first column like in the image.

entry.title: Order....: 1000820634 Residence time 1 not exceeded
entry.content:
Tried archiving the production order using report PPARCHP1 and PRARCHP1 as mentioned in link.

But got above issue. Have put deletion flag for the order. Any idea.

entry.title: [SAP Build Apps] how to disable dropdown field and change component style when disabled
entry.content:
Hi guys, our team is pretty new with SAP Build apps, so please bear with me with these simple questions --

1. How do I disable a dropdown field? Compared to other core components such as the input field, the dropdown field do not have a 'Disabled' property. Is there a way to disable a dropdown field?

2. For components that have a 'disabled' property, how do I change the style when the component is disabled? In the button component, when the button is disabled the style changes reducing the opacity by default. Is there a way to customize the style when disabled? For input fields, when the field is disabled, it does not have any visual change unlike the button. Is there a way to put some style changes when it gets disabled?

context: I'm trying to make an app wherein the user can only edit the fields when the toggle "edit mode" is on

thank you and hope you guys can help newbies like me!

entry.title: FIORI logon to app with default creditials
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have such a special case, business want to use small confirmation app without logon.

it is a simple app where is only possible to push button and confirm that the text was readed and it does not matter who did.

we created a technical user and in transaction SICF - > tab "logon data"  for Odata and for UI5 app set this user and password, so the app should be always logon with this user without requesting any other logon information.

But when we call the app the logon page occurs.

when we try to test just the OData service by GetEntitySet. we receive also the pop-up window for logon data, but in case we cancel the pop-up we can see the technical user is logon and the service return the data from Method GetEntitySet.

it seems like there is some higher lever authentification requiring the logon data.

do you have any idea what should be swiched on/off or should be set to avoid the logon pop-up or logon page?

entry.title: Schedule Accounts Receivable Jobs - SAP Digital Payments: Advice Processing
entry.content:
I am currently running the job Schedule Accounts Receivable Jobs - SAP Digital Payments: Advice Processing after the credit card settlement. I’ve encountered an issue where the bank key details are not being populated as expected in Schedule Accounts Receivable Jobs - SAP Digital Payments: Advice Processing.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Schedule Accounts Receivable Jobs - SAP Digital Payme...
entry.content: Refer SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3567963

entry.title: Burst a story using a conditional
entry.content:
I would like to be able to burst a story in SAC based on a condition such as Sales >= 10000. The recipients should get email delivery of the output that fulfills the condition. Could anyone suggest how this can be achieved?.

 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Cannot import a PD LM into ER/Studio using the MetaIn...
entry.content: Thanks for your help, Ondrej. The model imported fine after I deleted an invalid Inheritance object - it does not have a parent entity.

entry.title: Re: Extending business processes with product footprints using the Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4
entry.content: Would it also be possible to use this without SFM in place?  Certainly, it would be interesting to enable clients to start including CO2e information in the Purchasing process, even if they are not ready yet for implementing SFM!?

entry.title: Beyond Prioritization: Combating Alert Fatigue Through Policy Enforcement at The Edge
entry.content:
By Jay Thoden van Velzen, Technical Advisor, Office of the CSO

Cybersecurity teams, developers and system operators increasingly struggle with alert fatigue. As new technologies, regulations and security tooling are rolled out, this comes with an increasing volume of security scans and events. The more investment is made in security programs, the more alerts these programs generate. Consequently, this can generate more remediation tasks than the organization can manage.

For illustration, research has shown that organizations have only the capacity to remediate 10% of the vulnerabilities in their environments within a given month. Teams are forced to prioritize what is most important to address.

 

The Security Alert Prioritization Funnel

To manage the volume of alerts, security engineering teams filter and enrich data, add contextualization, allowing us to prioritize alerts by criticality and urgency. They automate alerts where they can, to reduce the number of alerts to something that security- or other operations teams – whether Security Operations Center (SOC) analysts, operational or developer teams – can act on. This funneling takes a lot of effort, so it is here represented as a mountain to climb.

Initial filtering can be done on the raw security data sources of events. Not all logged events are necessarily relevant. Neither are all data elements of the logged event. Data enrichment with ownership and organization, and metadata associated with the resource where the event was raised contained in asset inventory management further contextualize the event with technical and business context. This allows for the prioritization of alerts based on where in the landscape architecture the event occurred, what the resource’s function is in the landscape and how critical to business operation. It also assigns which organization is responsible for the resource and who to contact to act on the alert.

Automated runbooks for autoremediation, response or containment, that is, Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response (SOAR), can further lighten the load of the human operators at the very end of the funnel or buy them time.

Anton’s Alert Fatigue: The Study (2024) by Dr. Anton Chuvakin gives a good overview of this type of funneling within the context of security detection and response, and outlines challenges. But the same applies also to security configuration and vulnerability management, or alerts generated through security scans in the development cycle. All add to the alert volume that teams need to respond to.

 

Scaling Challenges

The problem with this model is that ultimately this scales poorly. Assuming that alerts are tuned well, with low false positives and appropriately assigned and prioritized, when the volume of the security events increases, this also increases the alert volume assigned to teams, and an increase in alert fatigue.

 Reasons for such increases are:

  • Growth in the size of the landscape and growth in deployed resources
  • Adoption of new technology platforms, such as public cloud, Kubernetes, or Software-as-a-Services (SaaS) solutions
  • Deployment of new security tools in areas not previously covered
  • Operationalization of new detection rules

To handle this increased volume of alerts at the end of the funnel, you can make the business case for more SOC analysts, developers or DevOps engineers. More likely you will be asked to do more with less. Automation, including with the help of AI, makes human operators more efficient and effective. But as the volume of security events increases, eventually you will be forced to an ever more brutal prioritization to get alerts back to a manageable volume that people can handle.

For illustration, let’s assume that we have 10,000,000 security events within a week that after prioritization and automation leads to 10,000 critical and high-value alerts, or a funnel reduced to 0.1% of the raw events processed. With 100 people to address those, that is 100 critical and high alerts per person per week.

What do organizations do when the events grow to 20,000,000, 50,000,000, or 100,000,000? Do they hire more people? Do they automate more? Can they afford to only address critical alerts, and accept the risk of deprioritizing the highs? Do they focus only on vulnerabilities listed in the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog? How much can you prioritize before your security programs become security theater?

 

Policy Enforcement at the Edge

The enforcement of policies at the edge, that is, directly acting on resources that generate security events, can reduce both the volume of events to process, as well as reduce the latency between detection and remediating action.

Reduce Event Volume

If we can’t prioritize and optimize our way out of the problem, we must “cut” the volume of events going into the funnel – or at the very least, reduce their priority. Applying policies at the edge that guarantee secure configurations or ban certain behavior reduces the volume of events that must be processed, while still allowing for notification that the event has occurred but has been dealt with. That gives high-fidelity signals that something has been corrected but doesn’t require immediate action from anybody.

One such example of policy enforcement at the edge, or in the fabric of the platform, are SAP’s cloud guardrails. These guardrails enforce baseline secure configurations of cloud infrastructure when resources are created or modified and do much of the heavy lifting in SAP’s Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) program. Among other controls, these prevent publicly exposed Secure Shell (SSH) and Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) connections and allow such connections from known SAP IP ranges. That protects against operators unintentionally exposing such ports to attack but also stops malicious actors from exposing such services should they have gained a foothold in the environment in another way.

Cloud account owners are notified when these guardrails auto-remediate resources, allowing them to correct their deployment scripts, or raise an alarm when they find they didn’t make the change that triggered the action.

Other examples of edge or in-the-fabric policy enforcement are Admission Control in Kubernetes clusters, enforced image scans and build checks. While those are primarily to ensure secure software development and deployment, they also ensure once deployed that adversaries can’t, for instance:

  • Deploy overprivileged containers
  • Deploy containers that don’t come from approved image registries
  • Deploy containers that lack required code signing

That adds defense-in-depth barriers that add obstacles to potential attackers, increases cyber resilience, and buys security operations time to detect and respond.

Reduce Latency

When human operators need to intervene, the time to resolve an issue can be very long. Research from Bitsight indicates that organizations in 2023 took nearly 4.5 months to remediated critical severity KEV vulnerabilities, and more than 9 months for high severity. That is in line with research from Mend.io that found that on average it takes 271 days to fix a vulnerability. Well-resourced organizations may be able to reduce that to weeks, but that still leaves a window for exploitation.

Response to suspected detected security incidents will be much faster. But in high-velocity landscapes, resources may only exist for hours, minutes or seconds, and be destroyed by the time a human operator can react.

 Automation through the funnel pipeline has a latency of its own, just to get alerts into the hands of the right people or to drive an automated response. The collection and ingestion of events, data enrichment and contextualization, and prioritization of alerts all require data collection, pipelining and processing. The higher the event volume, the more computational time that takes. Your SOAR automation may not be fast enough if it has to wait for that processing to complete.

Policy enforcement at the edge, on the host or in the cloud fabric, and before going through this event data pipeline reduces response latency.

 

Challenges and More Sophisticated Controls

Edge policies are blunt instruments. They operate before adding organizational context, and without the nuance that that enables. To take the public SSH and RDP example again, that guardrail applies to any cloud account and workload, whether running customer solutions, internal use by development, or a team running a sandbox account for experiments. Edge policies only work where their impact doesn’t create an operational problem for legitimate use cases or cause outages. Exceptions for edge cases can be complex to implement. The more varied and complex your environment, the harder it may be to implement a broad policy that enforces a particular configuration or bans certain behavior from the landscape.

The rollout of edge policies also requires more care. The cloud guardrails are deployed in four “deployment rings” where over the course of a quarter an increasing number of cloud accounts are onboarded, to minimize unforeseen disruptions. By first deploying to test and pre-production accounts, issues are bound to emerge during testing before they impact production environments, and the policy can be corrected or even withdrawn from the rollout.

Other controls can be too risky to implement. Forced autorotation of encryption keys or downgrade of authorizations for a seemingly overprivileged admin or service account can have devastating downstream impact. Auto-updates of vulnerable software components can have unpredictable consequences when not managed through appropriate development and IT operations processes. It isn’t possible to eliminate the funnel altogether.

However, based on its effectiveness in CSPM, SAP is exploring if such edge controls can be taken further. Aside from autoremediating misconfigurations and deployment controls, we are researching whether certain behaviors can be banned from the landscape. Technologies like eBPF allow us to reconsider what is possible. This is primarily used for observability through its direct visibility into the Linux kernel, including system calls and network events, for both IT operations and security use cases.

But eBPF also allows for the interception of system calls and network events, and therefore the opportunity to apply policies to block them at runtime. This could potentially ban reverse shells from the landscape, by blocking the setup of network sockets to a remote address with an interactive shell. It could block code execution beyond a process’s defined process boundaries and namespace and thereby protect against several classes of vulnerability exploitation.

Edge policies protecting against this, displaying clear malicious behavior with no legitimate use, would provide an additional layer of defense against the consequences of an attempted exploit, while providing a high-confidence indication that the affected resource is under active attack. This could trigger a deeper investigation by SOC analysts, but under lower time and impact pressure than if the attack succeeded – let alone if it had gone undetected.

We look forward to making progress towards this in 2025.

 

More Information

  • The Hidden Complexity of Vulnerability Remediation: Bridging the Gap between Data and Common Advice, Wade Baker
  • Anton’s Alert Fatigue: The Study (Medium), Dr. Anton Chuvakin
  • Asset Inventory Management Foundational to Effective Security Governance (SAP Community)
  • Bitsight Reveals More than 60 Percent of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Remain Unmitigated Past Deadlines in First-of-its-Kind Analysis of CISA’s KEV Catalog (Bitsight)
  • Securing the Software Supply Chain: Key Findings from the Mend Open Source Risk Report (Mend.io)
  • Cloud Guardrails: Effectively Making Your Cloud Accounts Secure-by-Default (SAP Community)
  • How SAP Automates Security Scanning and Alerting on over 6,000 AWS Accounts (SAP Community)
  • What is eBPF? (eBPF.io)

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub and SAP SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content:
Thanks Sandeep! I hope they are coming very soon, would be much nicer as you could instantly query the data as you need it. 

I found this influence request, maybe other customers would also like to have a talent intelligence API? https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#idea/330466 

Kind regards,

Simon

entry.title: Re: Business Data Validation of Custom Reports using Data Transition Validation (DTV) tool
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for the informative blog.

I have a question on the custom reports. 

Is it necessary that the person who has executed the custom report in ECC only has to run it in the S4HANA system ? 

While executing DTV for custom reports we are facing this issue where DTV is not allowing any other user to execute the report. Only the person who executed it in ECC is allowed to do the same in S4 HANA.

However, this limitation, we have not faced for Standard reports within DTV.

Thanks

Varun Arora

entry.title: Re: “Selection of Production Version” at the time of converting planned order to Production order
entry.content:
This requirement doesn't make sense because if I create a planned order with a production version of suppose '003' . When I am converting it into a production order I don't want the production version to be again selected automatically or manually . If that would have been the case then what's the point of selecting a production version at planned order level .

Later at the production order level, changing production version is of course an option

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Transformation with Dual Maintenance and Retrofit
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing, @Daniel_Enderli !!! Great information.
It would be good to clarify that you don’t need to implement FULL CHARM to use Retrofit, as there is a 'Standalone Retrofit' option available and this is transparent for the team; you only need to train the person responsible for the retrofit activity.

entry.title: SAP Fiori data conversion
entry.content:
Hi all

I have created a Fiori application using the template analytical list report. 
When I run my application in BAS the data is displayed correctly, however when running it in our test environment at the fiori launchpad, the data is changed. 

Can anyone explain to me why - and how to solve it??

 Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Step-By-Step Guide: Create trial account for SAP Integration Suite(2025) & Build an HTTPS iF
entry.content:
Insightful and helpful for beginners @Daggolu_PremSai !!

Keep up the good work!

entry.title: Re: Frequent Login Prompt for DM Refresh
entry.content: These timeouts are not related to the login prompt in a DM client. If your data-source is not SSO, it will ask for a user/pwd

entry.title: Re: how to handle unplanned freight cost
entry.content: thank you so much! we have a freight cost variance set up in AP, however, only $250 which was less than actual variance, I am looking for the best practice to handle the cost in SAP efficiently

entry.title: Measures sorting
entry.content:
hi all,

what is the best practice to customize the measures order in story?

for example, in the table below, i want to Switch position between the columns "percent" and "Total cost".

entry.title: Scheduling Data Load Jobs in Calendar Task
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working with calendar tasks and need clarification on scheduling data load jobs. Specifically, I would like to know:

  1. Is it possible to schedule, pause, and release the actualization of data load jobs within a calendar task?
  2. Can the calendar task be used to refresh the account dimension and dimension hierarchies automatically?

Any insights or best practices on this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: How do I turn off Learning access for all employees
entry.content:
We are leaving SF and I need to turn off Learning for all employees and Learning Admin except for the super admins. I have trying in my test environment and not getting the results I was hoping for.

Can one help me?

Nancy

entry.title: Re: What’s New Preview Available for SAP Analytics Cloud Q1 2025
entry.content:
Hi @JefB, 

Thanks for your question. 

The updated help for the Q1 2025 has not yet been released and will be delivered when the latest SAP Analytics Cloud is released. You'll be able to get the details of this feature then.

For now, use the roadmap link available with the description. If the link as I copied here doesn't work, please go back to the What's New preview and access there. The roadmap provides overview and benefit information of the feature.   

You can now monitor the jobs created while transporting the content. All jobs related to transport operations (such as Export, Import, Download, and Upload) are listed in the Monitor page. You can quickly review the import summary from the Status column.

See feature on the roadmap.

entry.title: Re: Order....: 1000820634 Residence time 1 not exceeded
entry.content:
Hello @RaghavanKathamuthu 

The blog you've mentioned is about mass setting deletion flag, not about archiving.

See the note 540834 - FAQ: Order archiving (PP_ORDER and PR_ORDER)

10. Question: During the initial archiving run (when you set the deletion flag and/or the deletion indicator) or during archiving, the system does not process all of the orders that correspond to the selection criteria. Why?

Answer:
Note that, in addition to the selection criteria, the residence times from Customizing of the order types (transaction OPJH for production orders and transaction CORN for process orders) is evaluated. In this case, the residence times have the following impact:

  • Residence time 1:
    The time interval (in calendar months) that must be between the setting of the deletion flag (for the determination of the residence time 1: time of the last change of the order) and the setting of the deletion indicator.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello all, 

Has anyone had an issue running the T4s via PC00_M07_CCYR with the Display Summary Results ticked, and in the middle of generation gets kicked out from SAP altogether? No ABAP runtime logs are being registered. 

I have logged in a case with SAP but haven't heard from support. If anybody has experienced the same, can you kindly share how you were able to get through this issue.

Thank you!

entry.title: I am getting problem Value is not applied during RFQ creation
entry.content:
During RFQ creation 

When I try to put Supplier address 

Then its shows Value is not applied.

Message No. DH864

entry.title: Re: How do I turn off Learning access for all employees
entry.content:
Good day Nancy,

You can achive that by removing the RBP "Access Learning" permission from the role(s) that is assigned to employees. They won't be able to access Learning at all.

You may achieve the same for users with Learning Administration by removing the RBP "Access Learning Administration" permission from the role(s) assigned to them.

You may need to maintain one RBP role and one RBP group for the super admins though so they still have the relevant "Access Learning Administration" permission in their RBP role.

Hope that helps, Frederic

entry.title: Re: Integration of Advanced return management with qualit...
entry.content:
Yes, it is possible to create an advanced return delivery (NB2) out of a quality notification (Q2).

Change the Function Module QMLR_CREATE_RETURN_ORDER (with an obsolete BAPI see note https://me.sap.com/notes/2492348/E) to QMLR_CREATE_RETURN_ORDER_2 an it works

BR

Jussi

entry.title: Re: Rollingup data for a Dimension in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: If you are presenting in Excel, please use excel VBA script to archive it.

entry.title: Re: HANA DATABASE
entry.content:
There is only one nameserver per HANA system (SID), and usually resides on the SYSTEMDB. 

Each tenant will have it's own indexserver.

entry.title: Re: Not able to preview app in BAS
entry.content: In BAS navigate to Menage(settings Icon) -> Profilies -> Tick the check box for [/] Use this profile as the default for new windows

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content: @hariprasadgud, the other one for csf job info is also created: Case 131552/2025 (P3) - Configuration transport center - CSF Job info issues.

entry.title: Re: Not able to preview app in BAS
entry.content: In BAS navigate to Menage(settings Icon) -> Profilies -> Tick the check box for [/] Use this profile as the default for new windows Now you'll get ride of this issue.

entry.title: Re: Unsupported OData version 4.01
entry.content:
Hi Uwe,

no, there is no UI5 version for that. The service URL looks as if the service is not meant for consumption on the UI but as an API.

Best regards
Mathias.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @mainak-aich ,

currently this is not yet possible. In the Custom Code Migration app you can navigate via the "Change Scope" button and get the list of the scoped/unscoped packages. Navigating from there to the content of the package via ">" will show you the desired object list for this particular package.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: TM Document Types in S4 Hana Public Cloud..
entry.content: Thank you Goevana and Thomas. It was really helpful.

entry.title: Ponting in period 13 (parameterization)
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Process:

I want to make a partial cancellation of an incoming delivery.

However, when I post this cancellation, SAP pops up this message.

I have reviewed the accounting periods

In theory I could count it because I have period 13 open.

Does anyone know what I would have to do to be able to account for this cancellation?

My idea is that by accounting for 31.12.2024, it is accounted for, but in period 13 in fiscal year 2024. I am a little "scared" that it is accounted for in fiscal year 2025 (current month) instead of in fiscal year 2024 as it should be.

Could someone help me with this issue regarding period 13 and how I have to parameterize it so that it is in fiscal year 2024 and not 2025?

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Ismael.

entry.title: Application preview in VS Code vs Deploy to ABAP
entry.content:
Hi,

We are creating ALP application based on CDS view with annotations.  When previewing the app in VS Code, it shows the value search help based on the annotations but when app is deployed to ABAP and accessed via FLP, it does not show the search help.  What am I missing?

Thanks,

Rutul

entry.title: Re: Webinar Q&A: How to configure the SAP Business One Service Layer for optimal performance.
entry.content:
Hi, @Balaji_S 

Thank you for sharing the questions that were asked in the English session, we look forward to the ones that were raised today in the Spanish session.

I would like to draw attention to a question that a colleague asked, regarding using one (1) Pro license to channel all requests to the Service Layer, the colleague asked if this was a bad practice.

I would like to know the SAP team's response, if I'm not mistaken, beyond being a bad practice, it incurs in improper use of licenses, that is, it would violate the EULA.

Thank you for today's session, I discovered topics that I did not know about the Service Layer.

Best regards,

Erick

entry.title: Re: Continuous Feedback Requests for inactive profiles
entry.content: There is no way to delete the feedback request notification on the system side, independently. We had to reach out to SAP and they performed a tailored script on their end to clear it.

entry.title: Re: How do I turn off Learning access for all employees
entry.content: Frederic thank you! I have been trying to test and this is the direction I was going but felt like there was more to do. I appreciate your time.

entry.title: Error text in SAP Service and Asset Manager 2310
entry.content:
I installed the SAP Note 3566662, because we had problems with the shown message. Now it works fine in the backend, the message shows which work order is locked by which person.

Our problem is, that most of our user don't have access to the backend. In the app itself the error message looks like this.

Unfortunately you can't see which work order and user is concerned.

Is there a way that the error message in the app has the same information as the one in the backend?

We are currently on SSAM 2310.

Regards

Philip

SAP Service and Asset Manager Mobile Application Integration Framework SAP Mobile Services SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM (Business Service Management) Event Status processing
entry.content:
Dear Support

SAP Cloud ALM (Business Service Management) event statuses are as follows:

  • Disruption
  • Degradation
  • Maintenance
  • Communication

How do you manage event status in Cloud ALM (Business Service Management)?

 

Best Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM (Business Service Management) Event Sta...
entry.content:
This KBA discusses this topic in depth: 3567887 - How to process SAP Cloud ALM (Business Service Management) Events?

In short, automatically created events (for Cloud Services) require no action from the Customer. They will be addressed on the SAP side and should close automatically. Manually created events (for on-premise Systems) must be created by a user in SAP Cloud ALM Business Service Management, and updated using that app. The events can be updated with new statuses and comments, and even eventually closed.

Regardless of the event type (automatically or manually created), event actions can be configured to perform some tasks in response to an event being created or updated. For example, e-mails or chat notifications can be sent to a group of users when an event is created. Or an operations automation can be triggered. Even ticket creation can be triggered via event actions (admittedly this is most useful for on-premise systems, where Customers can address the root cause). 

For further details and useful links, please refer to the contents of the KBA.

entry.title: Re: My app can not export PDF anymore after Windows 11 up...
entry.content:
As of CR 9 SAP no longer ships nor supports the legacy COM SDK's, that includes crviewer.dll and the RDC craxdrt.dll and craxddrt.dll which are all COM based. CRR does still use COM dll's but those are all used internally and in .NET.

Microsoft no longer supports VB 6 or any of it's dependencies. So rather than trying to HACK your way through your issues it's time to bite the bullet and upgrade to VS 2022 and CR .NET.

The same for CR 11.5, yes it may fix your issues temporarily but in the short term Microsoft updates may break 11.5, the technology is the same as 11.0, it just supported later versions of the OS. Windows 11 is not supported in either though because CR 11.0/11.5 had no concept of Windows 11.

To answer your latest question, again NO, as of CR 9 Crystal Reports no longer installed the .NET SDK nor the COM components you are asking about. And NO you are not licensed to use the Legacy SDK's any more, .NET is the only one along with Java and RESTFul SDK's.

So to answer your questions, stop trying to hack your way around upgrading, you can tell your users to be Windows Compliant you and they need to upgrade. The download page has a few .NET test app's I wrote when I work for SAP, retired now, which you can use to get your started upgrading.

Good luck

Don

entry.title: Re: Frequent Login Prompt for DM Refresh
entry.content: Hi Eyal, Marc is right, if your question is specifically about DM Office client prompts - SSO is the only option to avoid user-password re-entry.

entry.title: Re: Getting Cannot get/ error after deploying SAP BTP project.
entry.content: Hi All,

entry.title: SAC Excel add-in 'show unbooked values' by default - remove the default
entry.content:
Hi,

how can we permanenttly deactivate the new default feature 'show unbooked values' in that SAC Excel Add-in?

It disturbes the user handling.

Thanks

W. Ziegler

entry.title: New ABAP syntax, VALUE FOR skip appending entry if it already exists
entry.content:
Hello experts,

In the following test scenario:

I have an internal table that contains unique entries and duplicated entries.

Lets say we cannot touch this table and cannot delete duplicates etc.

And lets say I wanted to use the new ABAP syntax to get ONLY the unique entries from that table - how can this happen?

What I am trying is:

r_unduplicated_values = VALUE #( FOR row_of_tab_with_duplicates IN tab_with_duplicates
                                                        ( CORRESPONDING #( row_of_tab_with_duplicates ) ) .

What do I need to add to this syntax skeleton so that when a row has already been added like the one we are currently processing in the FOR loop it skips appending this row and continues with the other rows?

I know it is possible to just let it append the duplicates and then sort + delete duplicates but I wanted to do it all at once if possible with the new syntax.

 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Incompleteness check ship-to party (address) in VA01
entry.content: As already suggested, you should not consider address from an external source.  Always it should be from SAP.  That is how you should approach to this rather going for custom validation

entry.title: Re: How to show content is (not) available for mobile?
entry.content:
Hi @EmilyNS 

In the Enhanced Search, there is a library filter labeled 'Mobile Accessible'. By default, this switch is turned off, and the results will include all learning activities, regardless of their availability on mobile devices. When enabled, only mobile-friendly courses will be displayed in the search results. Please note that the availability of this filter is dependent on the activation of the new learning experience and the user's permissions.

This filter is also available when using the SuccessFactors Mobile App.

Additionally, when accessing SuccessFactors via mobile browsers, please ensure that users have access to a supported browser.

--Helpful Links--

Filters for Enhanced Search in Learning | SAP Help Portal

Enhancements to the New Learning Experience in iOS and Android Mobile Apps | SAP Help Portal

Supported Mobile Browsers for SAP SuccessFactors | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: I am getting problem Value is not applied during RFQ...
entry.content:
The error DH864: Value is not applied during RFQ creation occurs due to missing or incorrect supplier address data. To fix this:
1. Ensure all mandatory fields in the supplier address are filled correctly.
2. Check the supplier master data (XK03) for inconsistencies.
3. Verify that the country is assigned to the correct calculation procedure.
4. Ensure the company code and purchasing organization assignments are correct.

For detailed troubleshooting, refer to this SAP guide: [SAP RFQ Configuration & Troubleshooting] (https://help.sap.com/)

entry.title: Re: Getting Cannot get/ error after deploying SAP BTP project.
entry.content: Hi All, I am also getting the error Cannot Get. By following the tutorial, not getting the error however under Web Application there is no Fiori UI to access the application. Also getting error "Internal Server Error" when we open the Entities EndPoint under Service Endpoints. Can anyone help on this

entry.title: Re: An RPA-Driven Solution for Managing Obsolete Contacts in C4C & ECC via CPI
entry.content:
Hi @Siddhant04 ,

What was the need for using RPA here? I don't get it... Couldn't you just simply call ECC/ERP backend webservice/rest-endpoint using cloud connector from CPI to update the contact name in ERP? I saw you already get the contact name in line 16 of "RPA Payload creation (groovy script)". So my understanding, you could've actually added "del" string to the name right in CPI itself and then push the same to ERP from CPI rather than RPA..

 

best

Saurabh

entry.title: Re: Does SAC have a Rank function?
entry.content:
Hi @MadhuLodugu ,

You can do this just right click on measure section as shown below then

add client calculation as shown in below

After click on Rank Number on column will add to show rank

Kindly upvote and like if you find it useful.

Thanks
